name: El Tello and Els Cerros Loop Trail
name_original: Paraje Natural de El Tello — Circular desde Font Almaguer
description: Moderate 18.7-mile mountain biking route in El Tello, offering diverse terrain, pine forests, and panoramic views from L'Estepar Viewpoint.
region_intro: Mountain biking around Benifaió offers diverse landscapes, from the plains of La Ribera Alta to the elevated Montanyeta dels Sants. The region features an extensive network of trails, including routes that traverse agricultural fields and lead to historic elements. Riders can experience varied terrain, from gentle paths near the Albufera Natural Park to challenging climbs with significant elevation gains. This area provides a compelling destination for mountain biking, with options for various skill levels. Best mountain bike trails around Benifaió The most popular mountain bike trail is El Tello and Els Cerros Loop Trail, a 18.7 miles (30.1 km) trail that takes 2 hours 49 minutes to complete. This route offers a moderate challenge through varied local landscapes. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is View of Montserrat and El Real – Besorí Peak Fire Lookout Tower loop from Benifaió - Almussafes, a difficult 35.5 miles (57.1 km) path. This route features substantial elevation gain and leads to panoramic viewpoints. Local mountain bikers also love the Rio Magro Aqueduct – Water Fountain loop from Benifaió - Almussafes, a 40.9 miles (65.9 km) trail leading through rural areas and past the Rio Magro Aqueduct, often completed in about 4 hours 2 minutes. Mountain biking around Benifaió is defined by diverse landscapes, including the prominent Montanyeta dels Sants, agricultural fields, and proximity to the Albufera Natural Park. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult ability levels. The routes in Benifaió are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 50 reviews. More than 350 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Benifaió's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available in Benifaió?
A: Benifaió offers a good selection of mountain bike trails, with 39 routes specifically for MTB. These include 4 easy, 13 moderate, and 22 difficult trails, catering to various skill levels.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails around Benifaió?
A: The terrain around Benifaió is quite diverse, ranging from the flat plains of La Ribera Alta to the elevated Montanyeta dels Sants. You'll find routes traversing agricultural fields, gentle paths near the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/852349" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Albufera Natural Park&lt;/a&gt;, and challenging climbs with significant elevation gains, especially towards areas like Montanyeta dels Sants which features steep slopes.

Q: Are there mountain bike trails with scenic views in Benifaió?
A: Yes, many trails offer excellent panoramic views. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4489764" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ermita dels Benissants&lt;/a&gt;, located at the summit of Montanyeta dels Sants, provides splendid vistas of the Ribera Baixa, Ribera Alta, Horta Sud, southern Valencia, and even distant beaches. Routes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10352488" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Montserrat and El Real – Besorí Peak Fire Lookout Tower loop from Benifaió - Almussafes&lt;/a&gt; also lead to panoramic viewpoints.

Q: What are some natural attractions or landmarks I can see while mountain biking in Benifaió?
A: You can explore several notable natural features and landmarks. The prominent &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4489764" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Montanyeta dels Sants&lt;/a&gt; offers a unique environmental value and a historic hermitage. The nearby &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/852349" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Albufera Natural Park&lt;/a&gt; is famous for its vast rice fields, lagoons, and diverse birdlife, with viewpoints like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2439037" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Albufera Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt;. Some trails also pass by historic elements like old chimneys ('fumerals') and the agricultural fields surrounding 'La Torre de Mussa'.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes around Benifaió?
A: Yes, many mountain bike routes in Benifaió are designed as loops, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. Examples include the moderate &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e916054578/el-tello-and-els-cerros-loop-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Tello and Els Cerros Loop Trail&lt;/a&gt; and the more challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/39545866" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Besorí Peak Fire Lookout Tower – L'Estepar Viewpoint loop from Benifaió - Almussafes&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the best time of year for mountain biking in Benifaió?
A: The Valencia region generally offers pleasant weather for cycling for much of the year. Spring and autumn are ideal, with milder temperatures. Summers can be hot, especially inland, so early mornings or late afternoons are preferable if riding during this season. Winters are typically mild, making it a good option for year-round riding, though some routes might be affected by rain.

Q: Are there mountain bike trails suitable for families or beginners in Benifaió?
A: Yes, Benifaió offers 4 easy mountain bike trails that are generally suitable for families or beginners. These routes typically feature less challenging terrain and elevation, providing a gentle introduction to mountain biking in the area. The paths closer to the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/852349" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Albufera Natural Park&lt;/a&gt; tend to be flatter and more accessible.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy most about mountain biking in Benifaió?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 50 reviews. Reviewers often praise the diverse landscapes, the mix of challenging climbs and scenic paths, and the opportunity to explore both natural beauty and historic elements. The extensive network of trails also allows riders to find routes suited to their skill level.

Q: Are there any challenging mountain bike trails for experienced riders?
A: Absolutely. Benifaió is home to 22 difficult mountain bike trails, offering substantial challenges for experienced riders. Routes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10352488" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Montserrat and El Real – Besorí Peak Fire Lookout Tower loop from Benifaió - Almussafes&lt;/a&gt; feature significant elevation gains and technical sections. The Montanyeta dels Sants area also presents steep slopes that can be demanding.

Q: Is there parking available near the mountain bike trails in Benifaió?
A: While specific parking areas for each trail are not always designated, Benifaió is a town with various access points to the surrounding trail network. You can typically find public parking within the town or at common starting points for routes. For routes accessing areas like Montanyeta dels Sants, there is often a recreational zone with parking facilities.

Q: Can I access mountain bike trails in Benifaió using public transport?
A: Benifaió is connected by public transport, which can facilitate access to the region. While direct access to every trailhead via public transport might vary, you can often reach the town center or key starting points by bus or train, and then cycle to the beginning of your chosen route. It's advisable to check local transport schedules and bicycle carriage policies.

Q: Are there any specific regulations or permits needed for mountain biking in Benifaió?
A: Generally, mountain biking on established trails in the Benifaió area does not require specific permits. However, when riding within protected areas like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/852349" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Albufera Natural Park&lt;/a&gt;, it's important to respect park regulations, stay on marked paths, and be mindful of wildlife and other visitors. Always follow local signage and environmental guidelines.

faqs.question: Where can I park for the Natural Area of El Tello - Circular from Font Almaguer mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: The route starts and ends near Font Almaguer. While specific parking details for this exact starting point aren't provided, the broader El Tello area is generally accessible. Look for parking options in or near Font Almaguer, which is close to Catadau, a Valencian town.

faqs.question: Is the Natural Area of El Tello - Circular from Font Almaguer trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While the Natural Area of El Tello is a natural park, specific regulations for dogs on this particular mountain biking route are not detailed. It's generally advisable to keep dogs on a leash in natural areas to protect wildlife and ensure a pleasant experience for all users. Always check local signage upon arrival for the most current rules.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to mountain bike this route?
faqs.answer: The Mediterranean climate of the El Tello area makes spring and autumn ideal for mountain biking. Temperatures are milder, and the scenery is often at its best. Summer can be very hot, making early morning or late afternoon rides preferable, while winter can bring cooler, potentially wetter conditions.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for the Natural Area of El Tello?
faqs.answer: The Natural Area of El Tello (Paratge Natural Municipal El Tello / Paratge Natural Municipal Els Cerros) is a municipal natural park. Generally, there are no entrance fees for accessing such natural areas in Spain. Permits are typically not required for recreational activities like mountain biking on established trails, but it's always good practice to respect park rules and regulations.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this mountain biking route?
faqs.answer: The route offers a diverse mountain biking experience. You can expect a mix of surfaces, including well-maintained forest tracks, dirt/gravel roads (like the moderately popular "Amalaguer" sections), and some more technical, rocky singletrack. Some parts of the broader El Tello area are known for loose and very rocky terrain, which might require advanced riding skills or even dismounting and pushing your bike.

faqs.question: Is this mountain biking route suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: This route is classified as moderate. While some sections within the broader El Tello Natural Park might be easier, the circular route from Font Almaguer requires good fitness and some mountain biking experience. Beginners might find certain technical or rocky sections challenging, potentially needing to dismount and walk their bike.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest or viewpoints along the trail?
faqs.answer: Along the route, you'll encounter beautiful Mediterranean nature and pine forests. Notable highlights include L'Estepar Viewpoint, which offers panoramic views of the Vall de La Casella and the city of Alzira, and sections of the La Polvora Trail, Paratge Natural Municipal el Tello. You can also expect good views towards the coast and inland.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete the Natural Area of El Tello - Circular from Font Almaguer mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: The route is approximately 30 km long and typically takes around 2 hours and 49 minutes to complete by mountain bike. This duration can vary based on your fitness level, the number of stops you make, and the technical difficulty of certain sections.

faqs.question: Are there any cafes or facilities near the trailhead?
faqs.answer: The starting point near Font Almaguer is close to the town of Catadau. While specific facilities directly at the trailhead are not guaranteed, you can likely find cafes, shops, and other amenities in nearby towns before or after your ride.

faqs.question: Does the route intersect with other notable trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route incorporates sections of several other trails. You will ride along parts of Serra d'Alèdua, Camí dels Escopalls, Senda de l'Estepar, Puja dels Valents, Senda Estepar, Camí de Llombai, Senda Pla de l'Estepar, Senda Pujà de Potan, and Canyada reial.

faqs.question: What should I bring for mountain biking this route?
faqs.answer: Given the moderate difficulty and varied terrain, it's recommended to bring plenty of water, snacks, a basic bike repair kit (including a spare tube and pump), appropriate safety gear (helmet, gloves), and sun protection. A GPS device or a downloaded map of the route is also advisable.

waypoints.name: La Polvora Trail, Paratge Natural Municipal el Tello
